summary: Yongsan is Seoul's clearest structural swing district — the 2000 election was decided by 0.12pp, the 2020 by 0.66pp. In 2024, Kwon Young-se expanded his margin to ~4.75pp (51.77% vs 47.02%), bucking the broader anti-Yoon national wave. The 2022 presidential office move to Yongsan-gu is plausible local tailwind for PPP that the Korean Wikipedia source does not formally analyze.

issues.label: Candidate strength / incumbency
issues.description: Kwon Young-se has built personal brand across multiple cycles; DPK's 강태웅 is a credible repeat challenger. High-quality candidates on both sides define Yongsan races.

issues.label: Incumbency effect
issues.description: Kwon's expansion from 0.66pp (2020) to ~4.75pp (2024) is consistent with an incumbency bonus.

issues.label: Presidential office locus (2022–)
issues.description: Yongsan hosts the presidential office since May 2022. Plausible local attention / identification effect — but unconfirmed in sources used. Tentative.

issues.label: National mood
issues.description: Yongsan's swing-district nature usually makes it responsive to national mood; in 2024 the district resisted the national wave, suggesting candidate/local factors outweighed.
